name: Washington Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
renewalCycle: Every 2 years, on or before your birthday

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Washington pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Washington Department of Health lists a $140 original pharmacy technician credential fee and a $140 renewal fee.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Washington have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Washington. To work in Washington, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Washington?

acceptedAnswer.text: Washington does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing information page. Use the board application tools and provider search to check status.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Washington.
